title: DOCKET
subtitle: Dante's Office · 9:14 AM · Tuesday
epigraph_upright: Tuesday morning docket. The clerk has the cabinet open. The petitioner is in the anteroom. The bell has rung twice already. Dante at the desk, brass clock ticking.
epigraph_reversed: The docket is too long. The ruling doesn't come. The petitioner leaves. The widow leaves. The friend has nothing to say at the end of it.
scene_description: Standard docket day. Three appointments + the clerk + Nicola dropping by. Authority starts at 1 (Dante had coffee). Ledger at 0. The radiator is on too high.
direction_hint: Hear four matters. Land one hard mood. Stamp at least 3 rulings into the ledger.
opening_log_lines: the clerk is at the cabinet. the day is starting.
the petitioner is in the anteroom with a folder.
the bell rang twice. that means the appellant didn't come early.

stagnation_thresholds.label: the clock is running longer than it should.
people are looking at their watches.

doubt_thresholds.label: dante at the desk, fresh coffee.
he is not sure the ruling will hold.
he questions every page he stamps.

notes: Easy-mode Emperor. Tuesday docket. Four heard matters + 1 hard mood (the widow is lonely; the petitioner is preoccupied). Ledger needs 3 stamps. The clerk is a helper and doesn't count toward the connection minimum but DOES count toward the four-matter floor.
ambient_pool: the radiator is on too high.
outside, the bank-street is doing its tuesday-morning thing.
